summary: Mechanical formatting-only PR that ran clang-format across 36 files, mostly stage/ground source files plus one effect file and one header. Changes normalized include ordering, wrapped or unwrapped long lines according to project formatting, and standardized many repeated OSReport call layouts in stage object creation helpers. There were no PR body notes, review comments, or explicit reviewer feedback in the provided slice.
what_changed: Ran clang-format over a broad set of touched files: 36 files with 48 added lines and 78 deleted lines.

Normalized include ordering in multiple ground/stage files, for example moving <dolphin/os/OSError.h> before baselib includes in grgarden.c, grstory.c, grtclink.c, grtest.c, grtgamewatch.c, grtganon.c, grtpikachu.c, and grtpurin.c.

Adjusted local project include ordering in several figure and target-test stage files, for example placing "gr/grzakogenerator.h" before "gr/inlines.h" in grfigure2.c, grfigure3.c, grshrine.c, grtkoopa.c, grtmars.c, and grtness.c.

Collapsed many two-line OSReport calls into one line where they fit, especially repeated stage object allocation failure messages like "couldn t get gobj(id=%d)" in grfigure*.c, grgarden.c, grstory.c, and many grt*.c files.
Wrapped long declarations/comments when they exceeded format width, including the fn_802190A0 prototype in grpushon.h and a commented efSpecial_SpawnSpecial call in efsync.c.
Moved "sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.h" in grpushon.c from the external include block into the quoted project include group.
smart_moves: Kept the PR narrowly scoped to formatting, which reduces review risk and avoids mixing style churn with matching or semantic changes.
Applied formatting consistently across repeated ground/stage boilerplate, making future diffs in these files less noisy.
Let clang-format determine line wrapping for both active code and comments, avoiding hand-tuned inconsistent style in near-identical stage constructors.
Preserved existing symbols, function names, literals, line-number constants, and behavior while cleaning presentation.
decomp_lessons: Formatting-only PRs are useful cleanup checkpoints in decomp projects because they reduce future mechanical diff noise without changing matching logic.
Run clang-format before or after large batches of stage boilerplate edits so future functional PRs can focus on codegen-relevant changes.
Include grouping matters: clang-format may reorder headers within the project's configured categories, so future edits should follow the formatted ordering rather than local ad hoc ordering.
For repeated OSReport failure paths in ground/stage gobj creation helpers, prefer the project formatter's one-line call when arguments fit; do not manually split unless required by line length.
Long function prototypes in headers, such as callbacks or helpers taking Ground, CollData, mpLib_GroundEnum, and f32 parameters, should be left to clang-format's continuation indentation.
naming_conventions: No new naming conventions were introduced.
Existing address-suffixed decomp names were preserved, such as grFigure1_8020E080, grGarden_80202C78, grStory_801E30D8, grPushOn_80218ED0, fn_802190A0, and many grT* stage helper names.
Existing subsystem prefixes were preserved: efSync_/efSpecial_/efAsync_ for effects, gr*/Ground_* for stage and ground code, and mn* for menu code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or code-matching tactic is directly evidenced beyond mechanical formatting.
The PR appears intended to be behavior-neutral; it only changes whitespace, include order, and line wrapping in the provided diff excerpt.
Future matching work should avoid treating this PR as evidence for semantic changes in efSync_Spawn, Ground_SetupStageCallbacks, OSReport error paths, or stage callback behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, review records, or PR body text were present in the provided context.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available for this PR.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify these same grt*.c stage object creation helpers after clang-format cleanup.
Search for future functional changes to efSync_Spawn near the commented efSpecial_SpawnSpecial varargs call.
Check whether include ordering changes from clang-format affected any subsequent header dependency cleanup.
Look for repository clang-format configuration changes around March 2026 if formatting behavior appears inconsistent with older PRs.
